channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Functional programming in Angular
item.description: Chapter 1: The Problem with Mutable State in Angular Introduction: Why Functional Programming? Imagine joining a new project. You open the codebase, and this is what greets you: private client!:...

item.title: I just published my second NuGet package: Rehman.CurrencyTextify.Core
item.description: This lightweight and flexible package converts money amounts into text for 8 currencies: Dollar, Euro, Pound, Yen, Rupee, Rial, Dirham, and Dinar — now including PKR! ✨ Key Features: Batch...

item.title: Optimize your workflow with AI and save tokens!
item.description: Have you ever stopped to think about how much each question you ask Copilot, Claude, or Cursor costs? 💸 Often, our AI configuration files (.cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md) are...

item.title: After Effects vs Browser-Based Animation Tools: A Practical Comparison
item.description: Adobe After Effects is thirty years old. It has dominated the motion design industry for most of that time, and for good reason. Its feature depth, plugin ecosystem, and integration with the rest...

item.title: Why Most Banks Still Take Weeks to Approve a Loan (And How That Is Finally Changing)
item.description: Why Most Banks Still Take Weeks to Approve a Loan (And How That Is Finally Changing) The average mortgage takes 44 days to close. A small business loan can take even longer. In an economy where I...

item.title: Building a Simple REST API in Go (Without Frameworks)
item.description: Modern backend development often starts with a framework. But sometimes the best way to understand a language is to remove the abstractions. In this project, I built a minimal REST API in Go using...

item.title: NVIDIA AI Releases Nemotron-Terminal: A Systematic Data Engineering Pipeline for Scaling LLM Terminal Agents
item.description: NVIDIA's Nemotron-Terminal: Finally, an LLM That Understands Your Shell Isn't a Chat Interface NVIDIA trained a model specifically on terminal interactions — and the architectural decision to...

item.title: Implementing IndexNow Client in PHP for Bing and Yandex
item.description: When your content changes frequently, waiting for search engines to crawl is too slow. IndexNow lets you proactively notify Bing, Yandex, and other engines about new and updated URLs. Here's a...

item.title: Why Tool-Call Filters Aren't Firewalls: Understanding the Actual Layers of Agentic Risk
item.description: Most "AI firewalls" today are not firewalls. They are interface-layer interceptors—rule-based filters that sit between the model and the tool layer, blocking disallowed actions. Useful, yes. But...

item.title: Alena Rybakina: “The path to becoming a PostgreSQL committer starts with your first review”
item.description: We’re starting a series of articles about Postgres Professional engineers who received awards for their contributions to the PostgreSQL community. In this interview, core developer Alena Rybakina...

item.title: OpenAI’s Sora video generator is reportedly coming to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI's Sora video generator could soon become a built-in feature in ChatGPT, as reported by The Information. Sora is currently only available on its website or as a standalone app, which has...

item.title: WordPress debuts a private workspace that runs in your browser via a new service, my.WordPress.net
item.description: WordPress’s new browser-based service lets users create private sites without hosting or signup, turning the platform into a personal workspace for writing, research, and AI tools.

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News...

item.title: Rivian spin-out Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial AI-powered robots
item.description: The startup, which was created by Rivian founder RJ Scaringe, is looking to train on data from, and deploy in, Rivian's factory.

item.title: Alternative app store AltStore PAL is joining the fediverse
item.description: AltStore PAL, the alternative iOS app store available in the European Union and Japan, is joining the social web. In a post on Wednesday, AltStore PAL announced that users across Mastodon,...

item.title: Code Concepts: A Large-Scale Synthetic Dataset Generated from Programming Concept Seeds
Meta didn’t buy Moltbook for bots — it bought into the agentic web
item.description: Meta’s Moltbook acquisition may look odd at first, but the deal could signal how Meta sees AI agents shaping future advertising and commerce on an agentic web.

item.title: Canva’s new editing tool adds layers to AI-generated designs
item.description: Canva introduced a new feature that separates flat image files and AI-generated visuals into layered, fully editable designs. The Magic Layers tool is launching in public beta today in the US, UK,...

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command...

item.title: Amazon expands a program that lets customers shop from other retailers’ sites
item.description: The changes allow more merchants to participate in Amazon's Shop Direct program, which sends Amazon customers to other retailers' websites.

item.title: How to ditch Ring’s surveillance network
item.description: Recently, Ring ran a Super Bowl ad for its Search Party feature showing how it uses AI to scan footage from Ring cameras and video doorbells to help find lost dogs. It sounds neighborly — until...

item.title: You can’t replace the battery in Lego’s Smart Bricks — and many of its sensors aren’t available yet
item.description: The first Lego Smart Brick sets, based on Star Wars, aren't quite what my kids and I hoped, and I suspect much of that's down to programming. But the Smart Bricks may also have some technical...

item.title: Godzilla Minus One, a very good movie, is $15 on 4K Blu-ray
item.description: I love Godzilla Minus One. Granted, it’s only the second Godzilla movie that I’ve watched, the other being 1998’s terrible Godzilla featuring Matthew Broderick (the soundtrack has bangers,...

item.title: Meta Is Developing 4 New Chips to Power Its AI and Recommendation Systems
item.description: The MTIA processors are the tech giant’s latest attempt to build its own AI hardware, even as it continues spending billions on gear from industry leaders like Nvidia.

item.title: Canopii looks to succeed where past indoor farms have not
item.description: Canopii's robotic farms can autonomously grow 40,000 pounds of herbs and leafy greens a year while being the size of a basketball court.

item.title: I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job
item.description: Millions of people are on the job hunt right now - and for many people, landing a job in the AI era feels more intimidating than ever. That's why the onset of AI avatars running your job interview...

item.title: Tembo is a playful drum machine that thinks it’s a checkerboard
item.description: Tembo looks like a toy, but one of those bougie wooden toys you get on Crate & Kids, not some cheap plastic garbage. Despite its appearance, it is a fully functional drum machine and sampler....

item.title: Framework raises RAM and storage prices again
item.description: For the fourth month in a row, Framework is increasing prices on RAM and storage for its modular PCs due to ongoing shortages from suppliers. An update to Framework's blog on Tuesday states that...

item.title: Here’s where you can buy Apple’s budget-friendly MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is Apple’s newest, most affordable laptop. Our final verdict? It punches way above similarly-priced laptops in terms of design, and the performance isn’t bad either. If you can’t...

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the...
